drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.feature: Drop a matching marker file onto a loaded clip
drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.scenario: Markers are replaced when a matching sidecar is dropped
steps: Given a video is loaded with in-progress marker state
And a sidecar file exists in the same folder with saved markers
When the user drops the JSON file onto the app window
Then the video does not change
And all current in-memory markers are replaced with those from the sidecar

drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.feature: Drop a matching marker file onto a loaded clip
steps: Given a sidecar file is loaded over in-progress markers
When the user dispatches undo
Then the markers revert to the state directly before loading

drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.feature: Drop a matching marker file onto a loaded clip
drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.scenario: No sibling video found results in silent error
steps: Given a JSON file is dropped with no sibling video next to it
When the app tries to resolve the sibling
Then the error is logged silently
And the current state is unchanged

drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.feature: Drop a matching marker file onto a loaded clip
drop-a-matching-marker-file-onto-a-loade.scenario: A different sibling video loads with its markers
steps: Given a JSON file is dropped whose sibling video differs from the currently loaded one
When the sidecar is resolved
Then the sibling video loads replacing the current video
And the sibling's markers are applied
Given I have a clip i would like to export
When I check "Interpolate Frames"
Then A panel is revealed that lets me pick the interpolation method, including minterpolate and RIFE, and the target FPS,
Given I have a clip i would like to export
When I check "Interpolate Frames"
And Fill in 60 FPS in the provided input
And I click export
Then my output video will run at 60 FPS consitently, with interpolated frames to control the variable speed
hints: The "Interpolate Frames" checkbox and FPS input map to the WarpRequest field `interp_fps` (Rust: Option<u32>). When checked + fps filled, the backend re-times each segment at that constant fps using ffmpeg's minterpolate filter (blend mode) instead of just setpts.

steps: Given I have a clip i would like to export
When I leave options as default
And I click export
Then my output video will run at with PTS set to control the variable speed
hints: Default path: interp_fps is null, so each segment uses only setpts to produce variable speed (no frame interpolation).

export-options::bfc3070e.scenario: Export to a folder whose parents don't exist creates them
steps: Given I have a processed clip ready to save
When I export to a folder path whose parent directories do not yet exist
Then the missing parent directories are created
And the output file lands at the expected nested path
hints: save_to_folder (commands.rs) must call std::fs::create_dir_all on dest_folder before std::fs::copy. The Folder input in ExportDialog is free-text, so users can type nested paths that don't exist yet.

export-options::9a6cb1c6.scenario: Batch export saves each clip to the destination as it finishes
steps: Given I have three clips selected for batch export
And a destination folder is set
When the export begins
Then each clip is written into the destination folder the moment its render completes
And the next clip's render does not have to finish before earlier clips are saved
hints: In ExportDialog.process(), the dest-folder copy currently runs after the whole jobs loop finishes. Move saveToFolder() inside the job loop so each finished clip is copied to destFolder before the next warp starts.

export-options::fac99f99.scenario: Batch export continues past a failed clip
steps: Given I have three clips selected for batch export
And a destination folder is set
When the second clip fails to render
Then the first clip is already in the destination folder and is not removed
And the third clip is still rendered and saved
And the failure is reported in the export log without aborting the batch
hints: Wrap the per-job save+render in try/catch that logs and continues, rather than the current early `return` on error. Already-saved clips must be left in place.

export-options::d219ae15.scenario: The {beats} token in the filename pattern resolves to the clip's beat count
steps: Given the filename pattern contains the {beats} token
And a region with 32 beats is being exported
When the export filename is generated for that region
Then {beats} is replaced with 32
And the token is not left blank or replaced with the global loop-beats value
hints: applyPattern() currently passes `beats: loopBeats` for every job. For region jobs, pass the region's beat count (computed from its warped span, e.g. round((outPoint - inPoint) * bpm / 60)). Falls back to loopBeats only when there is no region context.

export-options::f6b6bac9.scenario: Show Folder button appears on the progress screen as soon as export begins
steps: Given a destination folder is set
When I click Export and processing starts
Then a "Show Folder" button is visible on the progress screen
And clicking it opens the destination folder in the OS file manager
And the button remains available for the rest of the export and after it finishes
hints: Add a "Show folder" button in the ExportDialog header/progress area that is visible while status === 'processing' (and after) whenever destFolder is set. It invokes the `reveal_in_folder` Tauri command against destFolder, not against a finished output file.

file-menu::36132c0a.scenario: A file opened shows up in recent files
steps: Given No files have been loaded
When File A is loaded
And File B is loaded
Then File A and File B appear in recent files list
Given File A and B are in recent files list
When Recent files clear action is called
Then Recent files is empty

file-menu::d1dd405b.scenario: Recent Files keeps up to 10 entries
steps: Given File 1, 2, 3 to 10 are loaded in order
When File 11 is loaded
Then Recent files contains files 2-11
Given File 1, 2, 3 to 10 are loaded in order
When File 3 is loaded
Then Recent files contains files in order 3, 1, 2, 4-10

frame-count-display::871cc353.scenario: Frame count shown next to timecode
steps: Given a 30fps video is loaded
When the playhead is at 2.5 seconds
Then the toolbar displays "75f" next to the timecode

frame-count-display::3a49f366.scenario: Step forward increments the frame count
steps: Given a 30fps video is loaded
And the playhead is at frame 75
When the user presses step-forward
Then the toolbar displays "76f"

frame-count-display::607ab793.scenario: Step backward decrements the frame count
steps: Given a 30fps video is loaded
And the playhead is at frame 75
When the user presses step-back
Then the toolbar displays "74f"

frame-count-display::fc7df5e5.scenario: Frame count rounds to nearest integer
steps: Given a 30fps video is loaded
When the playhead is at 2.533 seconds
Then the toolbar displays "76f"
Given a 30fps video is loaded
When the playhead is at 0 seconds
Then the toolbar displays "0f"
Given a 30fps video is loaded
When the user clicks the frame count display
And enters "100"
Then the playhead moves to frame 100
And the toolbar displays "100f"

list-selection::b8d2081f.scenario: Plain click selects one row and activates it
steps: Given a populated <list> list
When the user clicks a row with no modifier keys
Then only that row is in the list's selection
And the row's activate handler fires once for that id
hints: click → selection becomes [id]; activate fires for callers that map it to seek / set-active-region / etc.

list-selection::b99b4eb7.scenario: Shift-click range-extends the selection without activating
steps: Given a populated <list> list with one row already selected
When the user shift-clicks a different row
Then the selection now contains every row between the anchor and the clicked row, inclusive
And the activate handler is not called
hints: shift extends from the anchor (last plain-clicked id) to the clicked id; rows in between are unioned with the prior selection.

list-selection::1e9814c2.scenario: Ctrl-click toggles a single row in the selection
steps: Given a populated <list> list with one row selected
When the user ctrl-clicks an unselected row
Then both rows are in the selection
And the activate handler is not called
When the user ctrl-clicks one of the selected rows
Then that row is removed from the selection
And the other selected rows remain selected
hints: ctrl/cmd toggles the clicked id in the existing set; never activates.

list-selection::0763ac4b.scenario: Selection bar appears when 2+ rows are selected
steps: Given a populated list with two rows selected
Then the panel header shows "2 selected"
And a clear-selection (deselect) button is visible
And a bulk-delete (trash) button is visible

list-selection::152900a6.scenario: Per-row checkboxes appear when 2+ rows are selected
steps: Given a populated list with two rows selected
Then every visible row shows a checkbox
And the checkbox is checked for currently-selected rows
And the checkbox is unchecked for unselected rows

list-selection::0530ee04.scenario: Single selection has no checkboxes or bulk-action chrome
steps: Given a populated list with exactly one row selected
Then no per-row checkboxes are rendered
And the selection bar is hidden

list-selection::e6ae004a.scenario: Checkbox click toggles selection without activating
steps: Given a populated list with two rows selected
When the user clicks an unselected row's checkbox
Then that row joins the selection
And the activate handler is not called

list-selection::5287d418.scenario: Per-row trash removes only that row
steps: Given a populated list with three rows selected
When the user clicks the trash button on a different (unselected) row
Then only that row is removed
And the original three-row selection is unchanged

list-selection::da2c6dec.scenario: Header trash removes the entire selection
steps: Given a populated list with three rows selected
When the user clicks the trash button in the selection header
Then all three selected rows are removed
And the selection is cleared

list-selection::a6138eae.scenario: Delete key on focused list removes selection and focuses the survivor
steps: Given a populated list with focus and a non-empty selection
When the user presses Delete
Then every selected row is removed
And the row immediately after the deleted selection is focused
hints: Bound at the panel root via onKeyDown — caller's onDelete fires with the full selection id list, then the row immediately after the last deleted (or before the first if at the end) is selected and activated, mirroring a plain click on it.

list-selection::8e807736.scenario: Plain click on a clip sets both active and selection
steps: Given the clips list
When the user clicks a clip row with no modifier keys
Then that clip becomes the only selected clip
And it becomes the active region
And the player seeks to its in-point

list-selection::e190e98b.scenario: Modifier-clicks on clips don't change the active region
steps: Given the clips list with clip A active and selected
When the user shift-clicks or ctrl-clicks clip B
Then clip A remains the active region
And the selection now includes both clips A and B

list-selection::376277f3.scenario: Lasso on the timeline selects clips in the list
steps: Given the timeline with several clips
When the user lassos across the clip band
Then every clip whose [in, out] overlaps the lasso range is added to the clip selection
And those clips show an accent outline on the timeline
And the clips list shows checkboxes on each selected row
hints: Clip lasso writes lists.selection.clips; the clips list reads from the same store, so the timeline outline + the list checkboxes update in lockstep.

list-selection::e231708d.scenario: Lasso on the timeline mirrors marker selection in the list
steps: Given the timeline with several markers
When the user lassos across the marker track
Then every marker inside the lasso range is added to the marker selection
And the markers list shows checkboxes on each selected row
hints: Markers list selection is wired to warp.selectedIds via MarkersPanel's selectedIdsOverride — the timeline lasso has always written there, so list + timeline mirror automatically.

list-selection::51d95b99.scenario: Selecting in the list highlights on the timeline
steps: Given the clips list with two clips selected via shift-click
Then those two clips show the accent outline on the timeline overlays

list-selection::ecdd90c1.scenario: Selecting in one list does not affect another list
steps: Given a clip is selected in the clips list
And a marker is selected in the markers list
When the user selects another clip
Then the marker selection is unchanged
Given a list with three rows selected
When the user switches the list filter from All to View
And the filter hides one of the selected rows
Then the hidden row remains in the selection
And the rows still visible remain selected and checked

list-selection::388b677c.scenario: Plain lasso replaces the existing selection
steps: Given a populated list with two rows selected
When the user lassos a different range with no modifier keys
Then the selection contains only the lassoed items
hints: Plain drag clears selection before adding lasso hits; Ctrl/Cmd+drag is additive (snapshot-then-merge).

list-selection::320d6ceb.scenario: Ctrl+lasso adds to the existing selection
steps: Given a populated list with two rows selected
When the user ctrl+lassos a different range
Then the selection contains both the original and lassoed items

list-selection::564d68b7.scenario: Single-track lasso scopes to that track's items only
steps: Given the timeline with both clips and markers
When the user lassos starting and ending inside the marker track
Then only marker items are selected

list-selection::ed5022ee.scenario: Cross-track lasso selects items on every track it crosses
steps: Given the timeline with both clips and markers
When the user starts a lasso on the marker track and drags into the clip band
Then both marker and clip selections are updated

list-selection::cb8929f5.scenario: Right-click on an unselected clip pre-selects it
steps: Given the clips list with two clips and clip A is selected
When the user right-clicks clip B
Then clip B is the only selected clip
And clip B becomes the active region
And the context menu is shown for clip B
hints: Adobe-style "replace-then-act": opening a context menu on a row that isn't already in the selection first replaces selection with [id] + sets it active, so menu actions hit what the user visually targeted.

list-selection::0462dc5d.scenario: Right-click on an already-selected clip preserves the multi-selection
steps: Given the clips list with three clips all selected
When the user right-clicks one of them
Then all three clips are still selected
hints: Right-click on an *already-selected* row leaves the multi- selection alone — bulk actions in the menu still target every selected clip.

list-selection::a9ef63b3.scenario: Cmd+A in the clips list selects every visible row
steps: Given the clips list with three clips and none selected
When the user presses Cmd+A with the clips list focused
Then all three clips are selected
hints: Cmd/Ctrl+A bound on the focused list panel via useListSelection.handleKeyDown. Scope is the visible-items list — respects whatever filter is active.

list-selection::022db7fb.scenario: Cmd+D in the clips list clears its selection only
steps: Given the clips list with two clips selected
And the markers list also has selected markers
When the user presses Cmd+D with the clips list focused
Then the clips selection is cleared
And the markers selection is unchanged
hints: Cmd/Ctrl+D clears the focused list's selection. Other lists' selections are untouched (focus-scoping rule).

list-selection::440d0555.scenario: Timeline Delete removes the union of clip + marker selections
steps: Given the timeline has two selected clips and three selected markers
When the user presses Delete with the timeline focused
Then the two clips are removed
And the three markers are removed
And both selections are cleared

list-selection::9ca01b60.scenario: Timeline Cmd+D clears every timeline-side selection
steps: Given the timeline has selected clips and selected markers
When the user presses Cmd+D with the timeline focused
Then the clips selection is cleared
And the markers selection is cleared
And no items are deleted

list-selection::871d21c2.scenario: Plain click on empty timeline clears every timeline-side selection
steps: Given the timeline has selected clips and selected markers
And the active clip is set
When the user clicks the empty timeline body with no modifier keys and no drag
Then both timeline selections are cleared
And the active clip is unchanged
hints: Policy B from docs/INTERACTION_DESIGN.md — a plain click on the empty timeline body (no drag, no modifier) clears every timeline-side selection. Modifier-clicks are ignored so the user can ctrl-click without losing the lasso snapshot.

list-selection::5253b594.scenario: Modifier-click on empty timeline does not clear selection
steps: Given the timeline has selected clips and selected markers
When the user ctrl-clicks the empty timeline body with no drag
Then both selections are unchanged

list-selection::c16d9138.scenario: Timeline Delete also removes selected scene cuts
steps: Given the timeline has two selected scene cuts
When the user presses Delete with the timeline focused
Then the selected cuts are removed from the scene list
And the scene-cut selection is cleared
hints: Selected diamonds draw with an accent ring + a brighter through-line so the user can pick them out at a glance even when the always-show-scene-lines toggle is off.

list-selection::bc0146f7.scenario: Timeline Cmd+D also clears the scene-cut selection
steps: Given the timeline has two selected scene cuts
When the user presses Cmd+D with the timeline focused
Then the scene-cut selection is cleared
And the cuts themselves remain

list-selection::5afb8d06.scenario: Plain click on empty timeline clears the scene-cut selection
steps: Given the timeline has two selected scene cuts
When the user clicks the empty timeline body with no modifier keys and no drag
Then the scene-cut selection is cleared

timeline-navigation::2e61d3bc.scenario: While playing, Previous skips targets within 0.5s behind the playhead
steps: Given there are targets at 10, 20, and 30 seconds on the <track>
And the video is playing
And the playhead is at 20.3 seconds
When the user clicks Previous on the <track>
Then the playhead moves to 10 seconds
hints: Current onJumpPrev / onPrevScene filter with `< playhead - 0.05`. When `ui.playing === true`, widen that dead-zone to 0.5s so a target the playhead just rolled past is treated as "behind but still current" and skipped. When paused, keep the existing small (~0.05s) threshold so a press-and-hold at a marker still works.

timeline-navigation::4363a7f7.scenario: While playing, Previous still snaps to the nearest earlier target outside the 0.5s window
steps: Given there are targets at 10, 20, and 30 seconds on the <track>
And the video is playing
And the playhead is at 21.0 seconds
When the user clicks Previous on the <track>
Then the playhead moves to 20 seconds
hints: Beyond 0.5s past a target, Previous behaves as it always has.

timeline-navigation::030aad8c.scenario: While paused, the 0.5s window does not apply to Previous
steps: Given there are targets at 10, 20, and 30 seconds on the <track>
And the video is paused
And the playhead is at 20.3 seconds
When the user clicks Previous on the <track>
Then the playhead moves to 20 seconds
hints: When paused, the widened window is not applied — the existing small dead-zone (~0.05s) keeps single-frame nudges stable.

region-editing::2e73871c.scenario: Clicking a region moves the playhead to its start
steps: Given a region spans from 30 to 45 seconds
And the playhead is at 50 seconds
When the user clicks the region in the <surface>
Then the playhead moves to 30 seconds
And the playback state is unchanged
hints: Applies to both the ClipSidebar row click and the timeline region overlay bar click. On selection, seek the player to region.inPoint. Do NOT toggle playing state — if the video was playing, it keeps playing from the new position; if paused, it stays paused.

region-editing::9481d829.scenario: Right-clicking a clip in the sidebar opens a menu with Rename
steps: Given a clip named "Verse" in the clip sidebar
When the user right-clicks the clip row
Then a context menu appears with a Rename option
When the user selects Rename
Then the clip name becomes an inline editable input with the current name selected
And committing the edit updates the clip's name
hints: ClipSidebar row needs an onContextMenu handler that opens the existing ContextMenu with a "Rename" item. Selecting it calls the same handleStartRename(region) path the double-click already uses.

thumbnail-scrolling::90ecf3d8.scenario: Thumbnails start generating when a video loads
steps: Given an empty project
When [a video is loaded]
Then thumbnail generation starts in the background

thumbnail-scrolling::7498a0df.scenario: Filmstrip thumbnails are generated before background thumbnails

When the playhead moves to a new position
Then the frames the filmstrip requests around the playhead are decoded before scene, clip, and anchor thumbnails

thumbnail-scrolling::a010aa67.scenario: A region contributes a thumbnail at its in-point
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When a [region] is created or updated
Then a thumbnail is requested for the frame at the region's in-point

thumbnail-scrolling::ecab2b8f.scenario: Scrubbing the [input ruler] updates the thumbnail viewer

When the [input ruler] is [scrubbed]

thumbnail-scrolling::a02186dc.scenario: Filmstrip center slot equals the toolbar frame counter
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the playhead is at a time whose frame conversion is ambiguous due to floating-point error
Then the center slot of the filmstrip shows exactly the frame displayed by the toolbar frame counter
And the slot immediately right of center shows that frame plus one
And the slot immediately left of center shows that frame minus one
Given the thumbnail viewer is active and visible
When a thumbnail is requested for a frame whose thumbnail has not been generated yet
Then a placeholder is shown in its place until the real thumbnail is available

thumbnail-scrolling::10c1de68.scenario: Hovering a scene marker shows a thumbnail popup
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the [scene strip] is populated
And the [scene strip] is not expanded
When the user hovers over a scene marker
Then a thumbnail of the frame at that scene change appears in a popup

thumbnail-scrolling::07eab3fa.scenario: Expanded scene strip shows one thumbnail per marker
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the [scene strip] is populated
When the [scene strip] is expanded
Then a thumbnail of each scene marker's frame is shown inline inside the scene strip
Given a clip from 10 to 20
Then inBeatTime equals inPoint and outBeatTime equals outPoint
And clipin and clipout render at the same x-positions
And the clip is reported as default-linked

clip-bounds::2f5cddde.scenario: Setting either beat-space bound away from its input partner diverges the clip
steps: Given a default-linked clip from 10 to 20
When inBeatTime or outBeatTime is set to a value not matching its input partner
Then the clip is diverged
And clipin and clipout no longer share x-positions
Given a clip from 10 to 20
When the <edge>-point is changed to <new>
And the change is undone
Then the <edge>-point is at its original value

clip-bounds::7702c477.scenario: Setting in-point past out-point shifts the clip to preserve length
steps: Given a clip from 10 to 20
When the in-point is changed to 25
Then the clip spans 25 to 35

clip-bounds::4287ad02.scenario: Set-<Edge>-Point outside the clip creates a new clip
steps: Given a clip from 10 to 20
When the Set <Edge> Point button is clicked with the playhead at <playhead>
Then a new clip is created starting at <playhead>

clip-bounds::4473a9d1.scenario: A clip cannot resize below the minimum length
steps: Given a clip from 10 to 20
When the clip is resized to span <a> to <b>
Then the clip spans <c> to <d>

clip-bounds::c070411c.scenario: Dragging an anchor doesn't move a clip edge
steps: Given an anchor at inPoint
When the user drags the anchor
Then inPoint stays at 10 and only the anchor moves

clip-bounds::39ef3a66.scenario: Dragging a clip edge doesn't move an anchor
steps: Given an anchor at 15
When the user drags the clipin in-edge
Then the anchor stays at 15 and only the edge moves

clip-bounds::d342f900.scenario: Dragging a default-linked clipin moves its clipout too
steps: Given the clip is default-linked
When the user drags the clipin so the clip spans 15 to 25
Then the clipout spans 15 to 25
Given a default-linked clip from 10 to 20

clip-bounds::412c4541.scenario: A linked anchor pair conformed at <edge> displays the clipout edge at the paired beat time
steps: Given an anchor pair at orig <edgeVal>, beat <beatVal>
Then the clipout <edge>-edge displays at <beatVal>

clip-bounds::fa46649f.scenario: A clipin drag past a linked anchor temporarily conforms
steps: Given an anchor pair at orig 15, beat 15
When the user drags the clipin in-edge from 12 through 15 and out to 18
Then while the in-edge coincides with 15 the conform holds and clipout in-edge tracks 15
And as the in-edge passes 15 the conform releases and the edge continues with the cursor
And the anchor pair stays at orig 15, beat 15 throughout

clip-bounds::c9d92a3e.scenario: An orig-anchor drag across a clip edge temporarily conforms
steps: Given an anchor pair at orig 8, beat 8
When the user drags the orig anchor from 8 through 10 and out to 12
Then while orig coincides with inPoint the conform holds
And dragging orig past 10 releases the conform
And the clip's inPoint stays at 10 throughout

clip-bounds::0a12e07a.scenario: Clipin body drag onto a diverged anchor writes clipout to the paired beat time
steps: Given the clip spans 10 to 30 and an anchor pair at orig 20, beat 25 diverged
When the user drags the clipin body so inPoint sequentially reaches 15, then 20, then 22
Then at inPoint 15 pre-anchor inBeatTime is 15 and beat anchor stays at 25
And at inPoint 20 on-anchor inBeatTime is 25 and beat anchor stays at 25
And at inPoint 22 past-anchor inBeatTime is 22 and beat anchor stays at 25

clip-bounds::73129fa5.scenario: Edge resize onto a diverged anchor writes only the matching clipout edge
steps: Given the clip spans 10 to 20 and an anchor pair at orig <origVal>, beat <beatVal> diverged
When the user drags the clipin <edge>-edge onto <origVal>
Then the clipout becomes <clipoutResult>
And the anchor pair stays at orig <origVal>, beat <beatVal>

clip-bounds::7fe89303.scenario: Clipin drag past a diverged anchor with no output coincidence does NOT pull the beat anchor
steps: Given the clip spans 10 to 20 and an anchor pair at orig 10, beat 15 diverged
When the user drags the clipin body by 0.3 with the in-edge inside the snap radius of orig
Then the beat anchor stays at 15

clip-bounds::b1e19cfe.scenario: Clipout interaction on a conformed pair carries the anchor and writes the beat-space bound
steps: Given an anchor pair at orig <edgeVal>, beat <beatVal> conformed at the <edge>-edge
When the user interacts with the clipout
Then <edge>BeatTime is written to <beatVal> at drag start
And the paired beat anchor moves with the clipout <edge>-edge during the drag

clip-bounds::72147c44.scenario: Clipout edge drag on a fully conformed linked pair (orig=beat) moves clipout and carries the anchor
steps: Given an anchor pair at orig <val>, beat <val> conformed at the <edge>-edge of clip 10 to 20
When the user drags the clipout <edge>-edge to <newVal>
Then the clipout <edge>-edge is <newVal>
And the beat anchor follows to <newVal>

clip-bounds::9c1ac82a.scenario: Conformed-anchor drag tracks the clipout edge
steps: Given a clip's <edge>-edge is conformed to an anchor pair
When the user drags the paired beat anchor in output space
Then the clipout <edge>-edge tracks the anchor's position
And the lock-dependent value tracks accordingly
Given a clip with clipout length 10, BPM 120, lock=<lock>, lockedBeats 20
And the <edge>-edge is conformed to a beat anchor at <startBeat>
When the user drags the beat anchor to <endBeat>
Then <edge>BeatTime updates to <endBeat>
And the clipout length is 8
And BPM is <newBpm> and lockedBeats is <newBeats>

clip-bounds::c82bd950.scenario: Dragging the orig anchor of a conformed pair unconforms the edge
steps: Given a clip's in-edge is conformed to an anchor pair
When the user drags the orig anchor away from the edge
Then the in-edge is no longer conformed
And inBeatTime, BPM, and lockedBeats are unchanged
Given a clip with clipout length 10, BPM 120, lockedBeats 20

clip-bounds::3a7f5cd7.scenario: Clipout edge drag updates beat-time and the lock-dependent value
steps: Given the clip's lock is <lock>
When the user drags the clipout <edge>-edge to make clipout length <newLen>
Then BPM is <newBpm> and lockedBeats is <newBeats>
And inPoint and outPoint are unchanged

clip-bounds::1641fe80.scenario: Clipout edge drag carries its conformed anchor (inseparable while conformed)
steps: Given the <edge>-edge is conformed to an anchor pair
When the user drags the clipout <edge>-edge by any nonzero amount
Then the paired beat anchor follows the new edge position
And the conform is preserved with clipout <edge>-edge equal to the anchor's beat time

clip-bounds::7dc4ef19.scenario: Clipout edge drag snaps in output space only
steps: When the user drags a clipout edge
Then the edge snaps to beat anchors, other clipout edges, and the BPM grid
And not to scene cuts since scenes live in input space
When the user drags an edge such that <violation>
Then the moving edge clamps to <limit>

clip-bounds::c9282fbb.scenario: Clipout body drag translates both edges by the drag delta
steps: When the user drags the clipout body by 5
Then inBeatTime is 15 and outBeatTime is 35
And clipout length, BPM, lockedBeats, inPoint, and outPoint are all unchanged

clip-bounds::21c4a57d.scenario: Clipout body drag carries any conformed anchors on either edge
steps: Given the in-edge OR out-edge is conformed to an anchor pair
When the user drags the clipout body by any nonzero amount
Then each conformed anchor follows the matching edge by the same delta
And the conforms are preserved at the new positions

clip-bounds::b4892037.scenario: Clipout body drag snaps symmetrically — dominant edge wins
steps: When the user translates the clipout body
Then the edge with the closer snap target wins
And the other edge translates by the same delta

clip-bounds::500fafd7.scenario: Clipout body drag clamps to output bounds
steps: When the user drags the body past [0, OUTPUT_MAX]
Then the body clamps so both edges remain inside the bounds
Given a clip exists
When the user drags <draggable>
Then the BPM tick grid repositions to reflect the new position

clip-bounds::36d6e617.scenario: Changing lock fixes the new quantity; length is untouched
steps: Given the clip's lock is <from>
When the user changes lock to <to>
Then <kept> stays at its current value as the new fixed quantity
And the other quantities and clipout length are unchanged
Given the clip's lock is beats
When the user performs any clipout edit
Then lock remains beats afterward

clip-bounds::1b2c7389.scenario: Lock toggled mid-drag rebases the in-progress drag
steps: Given a clipout-resize drag is in progress
When the user toggles lock via UI mid-drag
Then the drag rebases to the new lock immediately
And subsequent cursor movement uses the new lock

clip-bounds::7869fac6.scenario: Direct BPM edit uses the grid model — length stays
steps: Given a clip with BPM 120, lockedBeats 20, clipout length 10
When applyBpmEdit is dispatched with newBpm 150, stretch false
Then BPM is 150 and lockedBeats is 25
And clipout length, inPoint, and outPoint are unchanged

clip-bounds::70f74525.scenario: Direct beats edit on a diverged clip changes length only on the clipout
steps: Given a diverged clip with BPM 120, lockedBeats 20, inBeatTime 0, outBeatTime 10
When applyBeatsEdit is dispatched with newLockedBeats 10
Then lockedBeats is 10 and BPM stays at 120
And clipout length shrinks to 5
And inPoint and outPoint stay unchanged

clip-bounds::7b7b6ec7.scenario: Stretch-mode edit on a diverged clip rescales only the clipout
steps: Given a diverged clip with inPoint 10, outPoint 20, inBeatTime 5, outBeatTime 15, BPM 120, lockedBeats 20
When <edit> is dispatched with stretch true
Then <changed> updates, <kept> stays, and clipout length rescales to 8
And inPoint stays at 10 and outPoint stays at 20
And inBeatTime stays at 5; the clip remains diverged

clip-bounds::2f896d39.scenario: Stretch-mode edit on a default-linked clip rescales clipin AND clipout together
steps: Given a default-linked clip with inPoint 10, outPoint 20, BPM 120, lockedBeats 20
When the user enters <newValue> while holding Alt
Then <changed> becomes its new value and clipout length rescales to 8
And the clip stays default-linked (inPoint stays at 10, outPoint follows to 18)
And inner beat anchors rescale proportionally

clip-bounds::f778bb71.scenario: Stretch-mode rescale considers only the active clip's anchors
steps: Given the active clip overlaps another in beat space
And a beat anchor falls inside both clips' clipout windows
When the user performs a stretch-modifier edit on the active clip
Then the shared anchor is rescaled as part of the active clip's clipout
Given a clip's in-edge is conformed to an anchor pair
When <action>
Then the in-edge is no longer conformed
And inBeatTime keeps its last written value
And BPM and lockedBeats are unchanged

clip-bounds::d3ae4f6f.scenario: Alt held during a clipout gesture inverts anchor-lock for that gesture only
steps: Given anchor-lock is OFF
And the user begins a clipout <gesture> gesture
When the user holds Alt during the drag
Then the gesture behaves as if anchor-lock were ON for this gesture only
And anchor-lock stays OFF after the gesture ends

clip-bounds::f864bd52.scenario: Clipout out-edge resize × anchor-lock × lock matrix
steps: Given anchor-lock is <anchorLock>
And a clip with lock=<lock>, BPM 120, lockedBeats 20, clipout length 10
And beat anchors at 12 and 16 inside the clipout window 10..20
When the user drags the clipout out-edge to make clipout length 8
Then BPM is <newBpm> and lockedBeats is <newBeats>
And the inner beat anchors <anchorBehavior>
Given anchor-lock is <anchorLock>
And a clip with inBeatTime 10, outBeatTime 30
And beat anchors at 12, 18, and 25 inside the clipout window
When the user drags the clipout body by 5
Then inBeatTime is 15 and outBeatTime is 35
And the inner beat anchors <anchorBehavior>
And anchors outside the original window are unchanged

clip-bounds::a0e7615f.scenario: Anchor-lock affects only the active clip's anchors
steps: Given anchor-lock is ON and the active clip overlaps another in beat space
And a beat anchor falls inside both clips' clipout windows
When the user pans the active clip's clipout body
Then the shared anchor moves with the active clip

clip-bounds::f280bc71.scenario: A completed drag is one undo step
steps: Given a clip exists
When the user completes a drag
And the user presses undo
Then the clip returns to its pre-drag state in one step

clip-bounds::5bbc1402.scenario: Cancelling a drag reverts state without an undo entry
steps: Given a <gesture> is in progress
When <cancel>
Then state reverts to the pre-gesture values
And the undo stack is unchanged

clip-bounds::1a0a5a8b.scenario: Reset Boundary clears inBeatTime and outBeatTime
steps: Given a diverged clip with inPoint 10, outPoint 20, inBeatTime 5, outBeatTime 18
When the user clicks the Reset Boundary button
Then inBeatTime and outBeatTime become undefined
And the clip is default-linked
And clipin and clipout render at the same x-positions

clip-bounds::6f35dd21.scenario: Reset Boundary is disabled on a default-linked clip
steps: Given a default-linked clip with inBeatTime and outBeatTime undefined
Then the Reset Boundary button is disabled
Given a diverged clip with inBeatTime 5, outBeatTime 18
When the user clicks Reset Boundary and then undoes
Then inBeatTime is 5, outBeatTime is 18, and the clip is diverged again

clip-bounds::e019060b.scenario: PointerDown on a clip's <target> activates it before any drag
steps: Given clip A is the active clip and clip B is not
When the user presses the pointer on clip B's <target>
Then clip B becomes the active clip immediately, before any movement

clip-bounds::fe042666.scenario: Active clip's edge wins a coincident-boundary hit test
steps: Given clip A and clip B share an x-position boundary
When the user presses the pointer at that x-position
Then the hit resolves to clip A's edge

clip-bounds::5f8fafd3.scenario: Non-active clip's edge wins when the active clip has no edge there
steps: Given clip A has no edge near x P
And clip B has an edge at x P
When the user presses the pointer at x P
Then the hit resolves to clip B's edge

clip-bounds::19ca8003.scenario: BPM-changing clipout edge drag excludes grid ticks and beat anchors from snaps
steps: Given a clip with lock=beats and a clipout edge being dragged
And a BPM grid line and a beat anchor are nearby
Then neither the grid line nor the beat anchor appears as a snap target
And other clips' clipout edges still appear as snap targets

clip-bounds::852b8ab6.scenario: BPM-changing clipout edge drag adds the self-clip's clipin bounds as snap targets
steps: Given a clip with lock=beats, inPoint 10, outPoint 20
And the clipout out-edge is being dragged in beat space
Then the clip's own clipin bounds at 10 and 20 in beat-space coords are offered as snap targets

clip-bounds::5c268456.scenario: Set-<Edge>-Point moves the nearest boundary when the playhead is inside
steps: Given a clip from 10 to 30 and the playhead at 20
When the Set <Edge> Point button is clicked
Then the clip's <edge>-edge moves to 20
And the clip spans <newSpan>
And no new clip is created

region-creation::30fd066b.scenario: New region size is the larger of 10% of view or 5 seconds
steps: Given the current viewport span is <viewSpan> seconds
When a new region is created
Then the region span is <expectedSpan> seconds

region-creation::089f7025.scenario: New region from the timeline is is aligned on the cursor position
steps: Given the current viewport span is 40 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 60 to 65 seconds

region-creation::622d79ba.scenario: New region from the region list is aligned on the playhead
steps: Given the current viewport span is 40 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
When a new region is created at playhead position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 60 to 65 seconds

region-creation::beaf3038.scenario: Region is clamped to the start of the video
steps: Given the current viewport span is 40 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position -0.5 seconds
Then the region in-point is 0

region-creation::220bf2e0.scenario: Region is clamped to the end of the video
steps: Given the current viewport span is 40 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position 119.5 seconds
Then the region out-point is 120
Given Region A is selected
When Region B is created
Then Region B is selected
And the viewport has not changed

region-creation::60eb8748.scenario: No scene markers at all — falls back to 5s / 10% rule
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 90 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there are no scene markers
And there are no other regions
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 60 to 65 seconds

region-creation::728bc8d1.scenario: Snaps between surrounding scene markers when both are in view
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 90 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 55 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 70 seconds
And there are no other regions
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 55 to 70 seconds

region-creation::3f2b3bed.scenario: No next scene marker in view — out-point stops at the viewport end
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 90 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 55 seconds
And there are no other regions
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 55 to 90 seconds

region-creation::a5661c96.scenario: No previous scene marker in view — in-point starts at the viewport start
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 90 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 80 seconds
And there are no other regions
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 50 to 80 seconds

region-creation::fe66bfde.scenario: No scene markers in view — region fills the viewport around the cursor
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 90 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 10 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 110 seconds
And there are no other regions
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 50 to 90 seconds

region-creation::dfeb811f.scenario: Previous region out-point takes precedence over an earlier scene marker
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 100 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 55 seconds
And there is a region from 60 to 70 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position 80 seconds
Then the region spans from 70 to 100 seconds

region-creation::373255c3.scenario: Next region in-point takes precedence over a later scene marker
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 100 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 95 seconds
And there is a region from 80 to 90 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position 60 seconds
Then the region spans from 50 to 80 seconds

region-creation::5d8f7863.scenario: Cursor inside an existing region — behaves as if the playhead is just past the region's out
steps: Given the viewport is from 50 to 100 seconds
And the video duration is 120 seconds
And there is a region from 60 to 70 seconds
And there is a scene marker at 80 seconds
When a new region is created at cursor position 65 seconds
Then the region spans from 70 to 80 seconds
Given [a video is loaded]
When the mouse is dragged horizontally along the [input ruler]
Then the playhead scrubs along with the mouse movement
And the video frame updates to match the playhead position

timeline-tracks::74c299b9.scenario: Click on empty area when nothing is selected
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user clicks on an empty area of the timeline
Then the playhead jumps to the clicked time

timeline-tracks::a6fb4913.scenario: Click on empty area when something is selected
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And there is a non empty selection of objects
When the user clicks on an empty area of the timeline
Then the playhead stays where it is
And selection is cleared

timeline-tracks::6eef362f.scenario: Right-click on the timeline opens a three-section context menu
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user right-clicks anywhere in the timeline area
Then a context menu appears with three sections: target-specific, track-specific, and global timeline actions
And global actions may be promoted to track-specific when the context calls for it

timeline-tracks::eff9cde8.scenario: Lasso drag within a single track selects its objects
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And markers are placed on the current track
When the user drags across an empty area of the track
Then the dragged area is highlighted as a lasso within that track
When the mouse is released
Then the objects inside the lasso are selected

timeline-tracks::da3524a2.scenario: Lasso drag expands across tracks when the mouse leaves the starting track
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And markers are placed on the current track
When the user drags across an empty area of the track
And the drag enters another track
Then the lasso leaves single-track mode and can span multiple object types
When the mouse is released
Then all objects inside the lasso are selected

timeline-tracks::6cee313c.scenario: Lasso across both boundaries of a clip selects that clip
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And clip 1 exists
When the user drags across both boundaries of the clip
Then the clip is selected

timeline-tracks::e840fcd6.scenario: Double-click in a track's empty area creates a new object
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the mouse is over an empty area on a <layer>
When the user double-clicks
Then the track creates a new <object> at the cursor position

timeline-tracks::bb2839e1.scenario: Double-click on an object in a track performs its primary action
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the mouse is over an <object> on a <layer>
When the user double-clicks
Then the track performs <action> for the <object> under the cursor

timeline-tracks::9fced362.scenario: Right-click on an object shows object-specific actions above track and global options
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the mouse is over a <object> in <layer>
When the user right-clicks
Then the context menu shows <actions> above the track and global options

timeline-tracks::89de8324.scenario: Right-click on an empty track shows track-specific create actions
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the mouse is inside <layer>
When the user right-clicks
Then the context menu shows <actions> above the track and global options

timeline-viewport::b69d7605.scenario: Wheel scroll pans the viewport horizontally
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user scrolls the mouse wheel with no modifier keys
Then the viewport pans horizontally
And the viewport zoom span stays the same

timeline-viewport::7d810b12.scenario: Shift + wheel pans horizontally even when deltaY is 0
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user scrolls the mouse wheel while holding Shift
Then the viewport pans horizontally regardless of deltaX

timeline-viewport::36bb2bb3.scenario: Ctrl/Cmd + wheel zooms around the cursor
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And the cursor is at horizontal position X on the timeline
When the user scrolls the mouse wheel while holding Ctrl or Cmd
Then the viewport zooms in or out
And the time at horizontal position X stays at horizontal position X

timeline-viewport::c35e82a4.scenario: Alt + click + drag pans the viewport
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user holds Alt and drags the timeline
Then the viewport pans by the drag delta
Given [a video is loaded]
When the user drags the timeline with the middle mouse button
Then the viewport pans by the drag delta

timeline-viewport::0c8056dc.scenario: Clicking the minimap recenters the viewport
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user clicks at a position on the minimap
Then the viewport recenters on the clicked time
And the viewport span is preserved

timeline-viewport::e1754e95.scenario: Dragging across the minimap recenters continuously
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user drags the mouse across the minimap
Then the viewport recenters continuously to follow the cursor

timeline-viewport::185ed2eb.scenario: Zoom is clamped to a minimum span of 0.1 seconds
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user attempts to zoom in past the minimum span
Then the viewport span stops at 0.1 seconds

timeline-viewport::40e55c56.scenario: Zoom is clamped to a maximum of twice the video duration
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the user attempts to zoom out past the maximum span
Then the viewport span stops at twice the video duration

timeline-viewport::ca7f472b.scenario: Viewport is always clamped to the video duration
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
When the viewport would extend before 0 or past the video duration
Then the viewport edges are clamped to [0, videoDuration]

timeline-viewport::f7ded0e9.scenario: Double-clicking a clip handle invokes Zoom-to-clip
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And a clip exists
When the user double-clicks the clip's handle
Then the zoom-to-clip action fires for that clip

timeline-viewport::67b26726.scenario: Zoom-to-clip fills the timeline with the clip
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And a clip that is not perfectly fit to the timeline
When the user invokes Zoom-to-clip
Then the viewport is set so the clip fills 100% of the timeline

timeline-viewport::96bc56e7.scenario: Zoom-to-clip toggles back to the previous view on a second invoke
steps: Given [a video is loaded]
And a clip exists
When the user invokes Zoom-to-clip once
Then the viewport zooms to the clip
When the user invokes Zoom-to-clip again on the same clip
Then the viewport restores the previous view

video-loading::90289e16.scenario: Viewport is set to the video duration on load
steps: When a video is loaded
Then the viewport changes to the length of the video

video-loading::ea78fa82.scenario: Viewport resets when a different video is loaded
steps: Given a first video with a long duration is already loaded
When a second video with a shorter duration is loaded
Then the viewport changes to the shorter video's duration

video-loading::c70c40ec.scenario: File browser is reachable before any video is loaded
steps: Given the application is open with no video loaded
When the user opens a folder containing videos
Then the Files panel lists those videos
And the user can click one of them to load it
hints: Regression — PanelDock used to be hidden until a video was loaded, leaving the file browser unreachable when a folder had just been opened. The dock now renders unconditionally; the empty/loading state lives inside the center column.
